整套首页
unit_25 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_25

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 366 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility
underestimatepessimismmanipulationwillingness
underestimate: standard unit headword | pessimism: standard unit headword | manipulation: standard unit headword | willingness: standard unit headword
#2 SE
The automation of many of the function performed at the factory, initially inspiring __________ in many of the company’s employee, has had none of the deleterious effects forecast either within or beyond the organization.The automation of many of the function performed at the factory, initially inspiring __________ in many of the company’s employee, has had none of the deleterious effects forecast either within or beyond the organization.
答案：A, E　A trepidation; E apprehension
trepidationapprehensionpathos
trepidation: standard unit headword | apprehension: standard unit headword | pathos: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious
celebratecelebratedoutmoded
celebrate: standard unit headword | celebrated: standard unit headword | outmoded: standard unit headword

In modern times, friendship has become a __________ relationship: a form of connection in terms of which all are understood and against which all are measured.In modern times, friendship has become a __________ relationship: a form of connection in terms of which all are understood and against which all are measured.
答案：C　paradigmatic
measuremeasured
measure: standard unit headword | measured: standard unit headword

The dazzling extent and variety of Pearigen's work (i) __________ those who search for artistic continuity; nonetheless, certain ingrained values are (ii) __________ throughout his oeuvre.The dazzling extent and variety of Pearigen's work (i) __________ those who search for artistic continuity; nonetheless, certain ingrained values are (ii) __________ throughout his oeuvre.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　confound; reflected
inconsistentappease
inconsistent: standard unit headword | appease: standard unit headword
#6 SE
Although initially it may be difficult to discern the essay’s message of peace and conciliation, a close reading reveals its essentially __________ nature.Although initially it may be difficult to discern the essay’s message of peace and conciliation, a close reading reveals its essentially __________ nature.
答案：A, F　pacific; dovish
pacificdovish
pacific: standard unit headword | dovish: standard unit headword

The company’s steering committee, reluctant to be held any specific commitments, released a strategic plan that was deliberately __________.The company’s steering committee, reluctant to be held any specific commitments, released a strategic plan that was deliberately __________.
答案：E　nebulous
commitmentrepetitive
commitment: standard unit headword | repetitive: standard unit headword
#8 SE
Citing the corruption and intrigue that pervaded politics in the city, my colleague __________ the newspaper’s trove of journalism prizes, declaring that finding great stories in the city must be effortless.Citing the corruption and intrigue that pervaded politics in the city, my colleague __________ the newspaper’s trove of journalism prizes, declaring that finding great stories in the city must be effortless.
答案：A, D　slighted; disparaged
politiccite
politic: standard unit headword | cite: standard unit headword

The new drug was useful, but unfortunately its effect was largely (i)__________ rather than (ii)__________.The new drug was useful, but unfortunately its effect was largely (i)__________ rather than (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　palliative; curative
curativeanalgesic
curative: standard unit headword | analgesic: standard unit headword
#10 TC TRIPLE
Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned
ongoingendangered
ongoing: standard unit headword | endangered: standard unit headword
#11 SE
Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield __________ solution.Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield __________ solution.
答案：B, F　an unconventional; an original
constructiveamicable
constructive: standard unit headword | amicable: standard unit headword
#12 TC DOUBLE
The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inadequate; secondhand
secondhandrepetitious
secondhand: standard unit headword | repetitious: standard unit headword
#13 SE
Such is the strength of randomized controlled trials that the debate among medical researchers is now centered on whether there are any situations where such a trial is __________.Such is the strength of randomized controlled trials that the debate among medical researchers is now centered on whether there are any situations where such a trial is __________.
答案：B, C　unwarranted; inappropriate
conclusivedefinitive
conclusive: standard unit headword | definitive: standard unit headword

That Seiberg and Witten lack celebrity can be explained by the __________ nature of their pursuit: the mathematical exploration of four-dimensional space.That Seiberg and Witten lack celebrity can be explained by the __________ nature of their pursuit: the mathematical exploration of four-dimensional space.
答案：B　esoteric
celebrity
celebrity: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　contribution to; oppressive influence of; quash
quashoppressive
quash: standard unit headword | oppressive: standard unit headword
#16 TC SINGLE
The governor might conceivably find a genuine resolution to the budgetary dilemma, but she may be tempted to engage in a deception: a __________ exercise in fiscal prudence.The governor might conceivably find a genuine resolution to the budgetary dilemma, but she may be tempted to engage in a deception: a __________ exercise in fiscal prudence.
答案：C　specious
dilemma
dilemma: standard unit headword
#17 TC DOUBLE
It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoarnerica. It must have been a (i)__________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)__________.It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoarnerica. It must have been a (i)__________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　protracted; unlikely
introduce
introduce: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed
validitymeasure

Blank 3

答案：A　incontrovertible
puzzling

#25 TC TRIPLE
Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles
politicparadise
politic: standard unit headword | paradise: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　disparities; variances

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　salvational; counter uncritical; salutary
underrate

This would produce a (i)__________ picture of scientific knowledge, with less (ii)__________ but more (iii)__________ results either relegated to obscure journals or left unpublished.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　distorted; dramatic; accurate
oversell
oversell: standard unit headword

Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)__________ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) __________ as the articles themselves.The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Blank 2
答案：C, F　unrealistic; informative

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　enduring; profit mongers; creative

Blank 2

#46 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating
regularity
regularity: standard unit headword

答案：E　inevitable
sequential

Blank 2

cagey

答案：A, C　abused; misused
misuse

#50 TC TRIPLE
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
evidentiary
evidentiary: standard unit headword
#51 TC SINGLE
In interviews, it is easy to mistake the novelist’s silences for aloofness, especially since in the court of public opinion all writers are guilty of being __________ until proven innocent.In interviews, it is easy to mistake the novelist’s silences for aloofness, especially since in the court of public opinion all writers are guilty of being __________ until proven innocent.
答案：A　snobs
wastrel
wastrel: standard unit headword

Their joy in movement is so pure, so complete, and so (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　delightful; contagious
delightful
delightful: standard unit headword

The concept of the Hellenistic period in ancient history has proved useful but also __________, with scholars disagreeing on the dates when the period began and ended.The concept of the Hellenistic period in ancient history has proved useful but also __________, with scholars disagreeing on the dates when the period began and ended.
答案：A, B　slippery; elusive
fruitless
fruitless: standard unit headword
#54 TC TRIPLE
It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
formality
formality: standard unit headword

This is neither praise nor criticism, neither a compliment nor __________, just an observation.This is neither praise nor criticism, neither a compliment nor __________, just an observation.
答案：C　an animadversion
exposition
exposition: standard unit headword
#56 TC DOUBLE
In his study of Senegambian cleric Shaykh Mass Kah (1827-1936), Bala S. K. Saho notes that it is difficult to fully (i) __________ many of the oral accounts on which the study relies. Saho's work shows, however, that despite this absence of (ii) __________, oral history can provide useful sources from which historians can reconstruct the past.In his study of Senegambian cleric Shaykh Mass Kah (1827-1936), Bala S. K. Saho notes that it is difficult to fully (i) __________ many of the oral accounts on which the study relies. Saho's work shows, however, that despite this absence of (ii) __________, oral history can provide useful sources from which historians can reconstruct the past.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　validate; corroboration
validate
validate: standard unit headword
#57 SE
Far from __________ innovations, as the patent system was designed to do, the patenting of concepts such as gene sequences give individuals and corporations a legal choke to hold over ideas that should be useful to all.Far from __________ innovations, as the patent system was designed to do, the patenting of concepts such as gene sequences give individuals and corporations a legal choke to hold over ideas that should be useful to all.
答案：A, E　spurring; fostering
codify
codify: standard unit headword

Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)__________ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)__________ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)__________. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)__________ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)__________ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)__________. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　operate outside of; active; report

rote: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　clement; likely

答案：B, D　predilection; proclivity

Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare; even movement from poor to middle class was (i)__________. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, "yielded rather (ii)__________ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America." So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare; even movement from poor to middle class was (i)__________. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, "yielded rather (ii)__________ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America." So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　an anomaly; pessimistic; obliterated

答案：C, E　unimpeachable; blameless

Blank 3

Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized

Blank 2

introduce: standard unit headword
#102 TC TRIPLE
Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell
oversell
oversell: standard unit headword
#103 TC DOUBLE
The strategists who created the European Unions practiced piecemeal social engineering. Recognizing that perfection is (i)__________, they set limited objectives and then mobilize the political will for a small step forward knowing full well that when they achieved it, its (ii)__________ would become apparent and necessitate further measures.The strategists who created the European Unions practiced piecemeal social engineering. Recognizing that perfection is (i)__________, they set limited objectives and then mobilize the political will for a small step forward knowing full well that when they achieved it, its (ii)__________ would become apparent and necessitate further measures.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　unattainable; inadequacy
measure
measure: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
A writing system that was not __________ but deliberately formed on the basis of other scripts was introduced under the ancient Indian King Asoka, who ruled most of the Indian subcontinent from c. 268 to 232 B.C.E.A writing system that was not __________ but deliberately formed on the basis of other scripts was introduced under the ancient Indian King Asoka, who ruled most of the Indian subcontinent from c. 268 to 232 B.C.E.
答案：D　indigenous
introduce
introduce: standard unit headword
#105 TC TRIPLE
Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain
endangered
endangered: standard unit headword
#106 SE
The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would __________ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would __________ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.
答案：A, B　combine; meld
meld
meld: standard unit headword
#107 TC DOUBLE
It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoamerica. It must have been a (i) __________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii) __________.It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoamerica. It must have been a (i) __________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　protracted; unlikely
introduce
introduce: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
In denying her a promotion, management cited her __________ decision making, charging that her opinions were based on passing whims rather than careful forethought.In denying her a promotion, management cited her __________ decision making, charging that her opinions were based on passing whims rather than careful forethought.
答案：C　capricious
cite
cite: standard unit headword
#109 SE
Joshua Gisemba Bagaka found that the pedagogical results of group projects and other engaged learning activities in Kenyan mathematics classrooms were __________; such activities, then, may not be the best way of improving mathematics education.Joshua Gisemba Bagaka found that the pedagogical results of group projects and other engaged learning activities in Kenyan mathematics classrooms were __________; such activities, then, may not be the best way of improving mathematics education.
答案：C, E　mixed; inconsistent
inconsistent
inconsistent: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
The characters in this comic strip fret about the (i)__________ of their “little counterculture lives”, especially when terrible things are happening in the world. But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)__________ in ways that do not seem (iii)__________ at all. Real things happen here – births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil union – and they matter.The characters in this comic strip fret about the (i)__________ of their “little counterculture lives”, especially when terrible things are happening in the world. But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)__________ in ways that do not seem (iii)__________ at all. Real things happen here – births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil union – and they matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　triviality; resonate; inconsequential

Research indicates that many people hold (i) __________ views about government, believing that elected officials should be able to overcome differences and get things done while also regarding a willingness to compromise as a sign of (ii) __________.Research indicates that many people hold (i) __________ views about government, believing that elected officials should be able to overcome differences and get things done while also regarding a willingness to compromise as a sign of (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　self-contradictory; fickleness
willingness
willingness: standard unit headword
#112 TC SINGLE
Not all Native American languages have lexical classes corresponding to those of other languages: many have no adjectival category, and some have even brought the __________ of nouns and verbs into question.Not all Native American languages have lexical classes corresponding to those of other languages: many have no adjectival category, and some have even brought the __________ of nouns and verbs into question.
答案：D　universality
universality
universality: standard unit headword
#113 SE
In mathematics, judgments about the validity of proofs are mediated by peer-reviewed journals; to ensure __________, reviewers are carefully chosen by journal editors, and the identities of scholars whose papers are under consideration are kept secret.In mathematics, judgments about the validity of proofs are mediated by peer-reviewed journals; to ensure __________, reviewers are carefully chosen by journal editors, and the identities of scholars whose papers are under consideration are kept secret.
答案：C, E　fairness; objectivity
validity
validity: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; forgo; benefit

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　rancor over; sensationalized

答案：A　tractable

Publicity around the celebrities’ donations to charity is often greeted with cynicism, but a study of the celebrities’ donation shows that they do __________ other donation.Publicity around the celebrities’ donations to charity is often greeted with cynicism, but a study of the celebrities’ donation shows that they do __________ other donation.
答案：C, D　draw; elicit

答案：A　ancient lineages

答案：B, F　irascibility; exasperation

答案：D　facilitated by

答案：C, D　quixotic; idealistic

There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it’s (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it’s just 80 or so—3 percent—that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there’s even one we’d like to see in greater numbers: Taxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it’s (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it’s just 80 or so—3 percent—that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there’s even one we’d like to see in greater numbers: Taxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　unfair; malign; blameless

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　ignores; transform

答案：D　marionettes

Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to __________ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to __________ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.
答案：A, E　hamstring; impair
impair

#130 TC TRIPLE
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predate that event

Nostalgia is to history as celebrity is to fame. Both nostalgia and celebrity are ways for the observer to (i) __________ things that are exotic, and that are characterized by their (ii) __________ the ordinary, and to make them, instead, familiar matters upon which the observer's own feelings can feed.Nostalgia is to history as celebrity is to fame. Both nostalgia and celebrity are ways for the observer to (i) __________ things that are exotic, and that are characterized by their (ii) __________ the ordinary, and to make them, instead, familiar matters upon which the observer's own feelings can feed.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　appropriate; difference from

答案：C　tendentious

答案：A, B　endemic; native

Carving materials such as stone, bone, antler, wood, and ivory were (iii) __________, but paper and drawing tools were unknown until introduced by early explorers and missionaries.Inuit print making is less (i) __________ than carving in that it does not have substantial historical precedents, although there are (ii) __________ incised carvings on bone or antler, facial tattoo marks or inlay skin work on clothing, mitts and footwear. 答案：A, D, G　traditional; affinities with; available locally

Scientists now recognize that the human brain has remarkable plasticity and potential for restoring lost function. Following injuries to the brain that cause permanent but localized damage to brain tissue, neurological deficits can often be (i) __________ by special training. In other words, changes in the brain can be (ii) __________.Scientists now recognize that the human brain has remarkable plasticity and potential for restoring lost function. Following injuries to the brain that cause permanent but localized damage to brain tissue, neurological deficits can often be (i) __________ by special training. In other words, changes in the brain can be (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　ameliorated; electively produced
underestimatemeasuremeasured
underestimate: standard unit headword | measure: standard unit headword | measured: standard unit headword

Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had __________ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had __________ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.
答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable
decisive
decisive: standard unit headword

答案：B　belied

disprove: standard unit headword

Blank 2

答案：A, B　minimal; trifling

Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)__________ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)__________ of tropical forests around the world. 答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic

It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique

答案：D, F　prosaic; pedestrian
manipulation
manipulation: standard unit headword
#208 TC SINGLE
A beaver's daily activity rhythm can be __________ the natural 24-hour light cycle; indeed some beaver colonies in Canada have shown a free-running cycle of activity that deviates considerably from 24 hours.A beaver's daily activity rhythm can be __________ the natural 24-hour light cycle; indeed some beaver colonies in Canada have shown a free-running cycle of activity that deviates considerably from 24 hours.
答案：B　decoupled from
impair
impair: standard unit headword
#209 TC TRIPLE
There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it's (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it's just 80 or so - 3 percent - that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there's even one we'd like to see in greater numbers: Toxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it's (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it's just 80 or so - 3 percent - that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there's even one we'd like to see in greater numbers: Toxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　unfair; malign; blameless
blameless
blameless: standard unit headword
#210 SE
Without a proper sense of its own legitimacy in Japanese society, the Liberal Democratic Party retreats to a sort of __________ politics that tries desperately not to offend anyone.Without a proper sense of its own legitimacy in Japanese society, the Liberal Democratic Party retreats to a sort of __________ politics that tries desperately not to offend anyone.
答案：B, C　timorous; craven
politic
politic: standard unit headword
#211 TC DOUBLE
Hyana Kusiemko and her colleagues speculate that the (i)__________ support among low-income workers for increases in the minimum wage is a form of last-place aversion: people who are in a marginally better position than the worst off seek to (ii)__________ to distinguish themselves from those in last place.Hyana Kusiemko and her colleagues speculate that the (i)__________ support among low-income workers for increases in the minimum wage is a form of last-place aversion: people who are in a marginally better position than the worst off seek to (ii)__________ to distinguish themselves from those in last place.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　tepid; retain their ability
willingness
willingness: standard unit headword
#212 TC TRIPLE
Arguments based on mere possibilities (i) __________ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii) __________ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as "Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about" can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii) __________ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.Arguments based on mere possibilities (i) __________ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii) __________ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as "Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about" can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii) __________ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　epitomize; polarize; decisive
decisive
decisive: standard unit headword
#213 TC SINGLE
From Neolithic pottery to twentieth-century posters, the extent and variety of the arts in China __________ those who search for continuity; nonetheless, certain enduring values are reflected in China's artistic traditions.From Neolithic pottery to twentieth-century posters, the extent and variety of the arts in China __________ those who search for continuity; nonetheless, certain enduring values are reflected in China's artistic traditions.
答案：C　confound
appease
appease: standard unit headword
#214 SE
The author argues that Thomas Jefferson was able to acquire Louisiana from the French primarily through a sustained show of __________, though it seems likely that an amicable approach would have been more efficient in achieving that goal.The author argues that Thomas Jefferson was able to acquire Louisiana from the French primarily through a sustained show of __________, though it seems likely that an amicable approach would have been more efficient in achieving that goal.
答案：D, E　truculence; pugnacity
amicable
amicable: standard unit headword
#215 TC DOUBLE
Computers have become adept in rarefied domains once thought to be uniquely human. However, they simultaneously have (i) __________ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii) __________ such fundamental skills truly are.Computers have become adept in rarefied domains once thought to be uniquely human. However, they simultaneously have (i) __________ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii) __________ such fundamental skills truly are.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　failed to master; impressive
outmoded
outmoded: standard unit headword
#216 TC TRIPLE
An innovative musician, accompanist extraordinaire, and protomodern hipster, Lester Young (1909-1959) remains a (i)__________ in the jazz firmament. Some of the reasons are obvious: although he dressed with conspicuous cool, he chose an artistic trajectory that seemed calculated to (ii)__________ the spotlight--his best work was in the bands of other musicians, such as Billie Holiday and Count Basie, rather than at the front of his own. Moreover, he was shy, subtle, and sensitive--traits not usually associated with great (iii)__________.An innovative musician, accompanist extraordinaire, and protomodern hipster, Lester Young (1909-1959) remains a (i)__________ in the jazz firmament. Some of the reasons are obvious: although he dressed with conspicuous cool, he chose an artistic trajectory that seemed calculated to (ii)__________ the spotlight--his best work was in the bands of other musicians, such as Billie Holiday and Count Basie, rather than at the front of his own. Moreover, he was shy, subtle, and sensitive--traits not usually associated with great (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　lesser light; shun; celebrity
celebrity
celebrity: standard unit headword
#217 TC SINGLE
Apocalyptic ideology can be __________ in its effects, since those who predict the end of the world have often been great innovators: the fear of catastrophe, despite its perceived inevitability, frequently acts as a spur to construction and renewal.Apocalyptic ideology can be __________ in its effects, since those who predict the end of the world have often been great innovators: the fear of catastrophe, despite its perceived inevitability, frequently acts as a spur to construction and renewal.
答案：C　paradoxical
tendentious
tendentious: standard unit headword
#218 SE
The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market's sometimes destructive vicissitudes being __________ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market's sometimes destructive vicissitudes being __________ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.
答案：A, E　tempered; mitigated
politic
politic: standard unit headword
#219 TC DOUBLE
She was skeptical about the (i) __________ reviews of the latest production of the play, feeling that the production she had seen years earlier was (ii) __________, and that any successive attempts must undoubtedly fall short.She was skeptical about the (i) __________ reviews of the latest production of the play, feeling that the production she had seen years earlier was (ii) __________, and that any successive attempts must undoubtedly fall short.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rhapsodic; nothing less than definitive
definitive

#220 TC SINGLE
Scientists have made progress towards developing a nuclear-fusion reactor to provide energy, but they are still far from reaching this goal, which sometimes seems to __________ despite significant advances made by the scientists.Scientists have made progress towards developing a nuclear-fusion reactor to provide energy, but they are still far from reaching this goal, which sometimes seems to __________ despite significant advances made by the scientists.
答案：C　recede
worsen
worsen: standard unit headword

In the past, scholars viewed emotion as a natural and essential force that (ii)__________ analysis - a strictly private matter - and therefore (iii)__________ social life and the stuff of research.It is only recently that emotion has attracted any substantial scholarly attention, with historians lagging behind anthropologists, sociologists, and philosophers in their willingness to (i)__________ emotion as a subject worthy of scholarly attention. In the past, scholars viewed emotion as a natural and essential force that (ii)__________ analysis - a strictly private matter - and therefore (iii)__________ social life and the stuff of research.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　recognize; defied; extraneous to
willingness

The lecturer (i) __________ to provide innovative insights into important issues, but her promise of originality was more than she delivered: most of her pronouncements were (ii) __________.The lecturer (i) __________ to provide innovative insights into important issues, but her promise of originality was more than she delivered: most of her pronouncements were (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undertook; platitudinous
platitudinous
platitudinous: standard unit headword
#223 SE
Muriel Spark suggested that Mary Shelley's prose style was often verbose and platitudinous, the very opposite of Spark's favored __________.Muriel Spark suggested that Mary Shelley's prose style was often verbose and platitudinous, the very opposite of Spark's favored __________.
答案：E, F　concision; pithiness
platitudinous
platitudinous: standard unit headword
#224 TC SINGLE
With the numerous opponents of the controversial new taxation measure in such a fury, anyone who publicly advocated the measure did not fail to meet with __________ usage.With the numerous opponents of the controversial new taxation measure in such a fury, anyone who publicly advocated the measure did not fail to meet with __________ usage.
答案：B　severe
measurepolitic
measure: standard unit headword | politic: standard unit headword
#225 TC TRIPLE
Despite the (i)__________ in negotiations apparently signaled by the recent agreement between the two neighboring countries, the countries remain (ii)__________ even about the import of that agreement. One wants to prolong agreed-to resumption of limited cross-border traffic, believing it can become entrenched as a new status quo. The other, by contrast, insists that the resumption is (iii)__________ and has been undertaken voluntarily and provisionally in order to show goodwill.Despite the (i)__________ in negotiations apparently signaled by the recent agreement between the two neighboring countries, the countries remain (ii)__________ even about the import of that agreement. One wants to prolong agreed-to resumption of limited cross-border traffic, believing it can become entrenched as a new status quo. The other, by contrast, insists that the resumption is (iii)__________ and has been undertaken voluntarily and provisionally in order to show goodwill.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　progress; unreconciled; temporary
import
import: standard unit headword
#226 SE
Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.
答案：B, D　prescribe; codify
codify
codify: standard unit headword
#227 TC DOUBLE
Among wide-ranging animal species, populations at the edge of the species' range are frequently exposed to less (i) __________ and more variable conditions than those in other parts of the range. As a result, the animal's abundance is often (ii) __________.Among wide-ranging animal species, populations at the edge of the species' range are frequently exposed to less (i) __________ and more variable conditions than those in other parts of the range. As a result, the animal's abundance is often (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　favorable; lower at the periphery
underestimate
underestimate: standard unit headword
#228 TC TRIPLE
It is inevitable that ongoing research presupposes some accepted science as a principle. Usually no one even notices the implication that the accepted principle is being (i)__________ unless there emerges (ii)__________ that turns out to be sufficiently (iii)__________ that the assumptions underlying the research come to be considered.It is inevitable that ongoing research presupposes some accepted science as a principle. Usually no one even notices the implication that the accepted principle is being (i)__________ unless there emerges (ii)__________ that turns out to be sufficiently (iii)__________ that the assumptions underlying the research come to be considered.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　tested; an anomaly; recalcitrant
ongoing
ongoing: standard unit headword
#229 SE
Brief personal impressions are rarely __________, especially when recalled after many years; but the author's initial impression of Gellner as an exceptionally honest thinker is beyond reasonable doubt.Brief personal impressions are rarely __________, especially when recalled after many years; but the author's initial impression of Gellner as an exceptionally honest thinker is beyond reasonable doubt.
答案：B, E　definitive; authoritative
definitive
definitive: standard unit headword
#230 TC DOUBLE
It would be naive to treat remarks made in diaries or personal letters as giving especially candid access to historical truth or even as being expressions of the writer's true state of mind, since (i) __________ for exaggeration and deception in those forms is virtually nonexistent. Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii) __________.It would be naive to treat remarks made in diaries or personal letters as giving especially candid access to historical truth or even as being expressions of the writer's true state of mind, since (i) __________ for exaggeration and deception in those forms is virtually nonexistent. Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　penalty; balanced reflection

#231 TC TRIPLE
Genetic researchers have recently suggested that our political leanings may be determined by our DNA, a view that tends to undermine our reflective self-flattery. We (i)__________ the idea that personal politics are entirely (ii)__________. The genetic explanation for ideology (iii)__________ our belief that we are persuaded only by rational arguments.Genetic researchers have recently suggested that our political leanings may be determined by our DNA, a view that tends to undermine our reflective self-flattery. We (i)__________ the idea that personal politics are entirely (ii)__________. The genetic explanation for ideology (iii)__________ our belief that we are persuaded only by rational arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　revel in; deliberative; defies

The climate researchers' __________ method of presentation caused a stir among those who felt that scientific papers should be dispassionate in their delivery of evidence.The climate researchers' __________ method of presentation caused a stir among those who felt that scientific papers should be dispassionate in their delivery of evidence.
答案：A, C　prejudicial; tendentious

tendentious: standard unit headword
#233 TC DOUBLE
Recent scholarship has questioned the (i) __________ of tropical forests around the world. Archaeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa's savanna forest transition zone are (ii) __________ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i) __________ of tropical forests around the world. Archaeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa's savanna forest transition zone are (ii) __________ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic
endangered
endangered: standard unit headword
#234 TC TRIPLE
It appears that the terrestrial biosphere has been absorbing a significant fraction of anthropogenic CO2, thereby slowing down the rate of global climate change. However, the (i) __________ of this (ii) __________ is not (iii) __________: if pushed too far, the biosphere might become a net source of CO2, thereby accelerating climate change and causing a positive feedback loop.It appears that the terrestrial biosphere has been absorbing a significant fraction of anthropogenic CO2, thereby slowing down the rate of global climate change. However, the (i) __________ of this (ii) __________ is not (iii) __________: if pushed too far, the biosphere might become a net source of CO2, thereby accelerating climate change and causing a positive feedback loop.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　continuation; compensatory mechanism; guaranteed
absorbing
absorbing: standard unit headword
#235 SE
By avoiding hyperbole in her definitive account of one of the most influential but controversial banks of the modern era, Hurst shows herself to be a __________ author.By avoiding hyperbole in her definitive account of one of the most influential but controversial banks of the modern era, Hurst shows herself to be a __________ author.
答案：A, D　judicious; sensible
definitive
definitive: standard unit headword
#236 TC DOUBLE
Scientists generally consider a wholesale melting of the Greenland or West Antarctic ice sheets in the twenty-first century unlikely, even if global warming continues. Nevertheless, because scientific knowledge of the dynamics of land ice sheets is (i) __________, there is a possibility that the risk of significant melting of land ice (ii) __________.Scientists generally consider a wholesale melting of the Greenland or West Antarctic ice sheets in the twenty-first century unlikely, even if global warming continues. Nevertheless, because scientific knowledge of the dynamics of land ice sheets is (i) __________, there is a possibility that the risk of significant melting of land ice (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　rudimentary; has been underestimated
underestimate

#237 TC TRIPLE
For years, wine writers have (i) __________ the public's (ii) __________ the manifest charms of sherry. Why doesn't everyone love sherry, they asked, celebrating it as the most (iii) __________ wine in the world.For years, wine writers have (i) __________ the public's (ii) __________ the manifest charms of sherry. Why doesn't everyone love sherry, they asked, celebrating it as the most (iii) __________ wine in the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

#238 SE
One object of examining past medical practices is to identify useful knowledge amid its now outmoded trappings; the alternative is to let such knowledge __________ along with the discredited theories with which it was associated.One object of examining past medical practices is to identify useful knowledge amid its now outmoded trappings; the alternative is to let such knowledge __________ along with the discredited theories with which it was associated.
答案：A, D　fade; wane

#239 TC DOUBLE
In talking to reporters about the controversial legislation before Congress, the senator seemed thoughtful and even-handed, but when he supported that legislation in a subsequent Senate floor speech, his approach was (i) __________, as though he regarded opposition to the bill as (ii) __________.In talking to reporters about the controversial legislation before Congress, the senator seemed thoughtful and even-handed, but when he supported that legislation in a subsequent Senate floor speech, his approach was (i) __________, as though he regarded opposition to the bill as (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　tendentious; perverse
tendentious
tendentious: standard unit headword
#240 TC TRIPLE
Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work, (i) __________ to propose measures that might (ii) __________ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii) __________, they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work, (i) __________ to propose measures that might (ii) __________ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii) __________, they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　forgoing opportunities; ameliorate; reticence
measure
measure: standard unit headword
#241 SE
In his article, Bazerman highlights Edison's __________ manipulation of the press, including gifts of stock to friendly reporters.In his article, Bazerman highlights Edison's __________ manipulation of the press, including gifts of stock to friendly reporters.
答案：D, F　brazen; barefaced

They typically seek to (ii)__________ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)__________. They typically seek to (ii)__________ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　doubt their own legitimacy; validate

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　retain; condemnation of; forgo

My colleagues __________ the journalist's vaunted expertise at securing high-profile interviews, declaring that, given her family's connections to celebrities, obtaining such interviews must be no more difficult than asking a friend out for coffee.My colleagues __________ the journalist's vaunted expertise at securing high-profile interviews, declaring that, given her family's connections to celebrities, obtaining such interviews must be no more difficult than asking a friend out for coffee.

Those experts in classifying species tend to be (ii) __________ North America and Europe, whereas most of the undocumented biodiversity is likely in the tropics.The documentation of Earth's biodiversity is complicated by the (i) __________ taxonomists. Those experts in classifying species tend to be (ii) __________ North America and Europe, whereas most of the undocumented biodiversity is likely in the tropics.
Blank 1
Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 2

Moreover, Earth Day--a global holiday meant to celebrate the wonder of life on the planet--has (ii) __________ the frivolous "picnic holidays," its significance (iii) __________ the smoke of barbecue pits and backyard grills.

Blank 2
Blank 3

In a competitive news market, producers can use slant to differentiate their products and stave off price competition.If newspaper consumers are concerned about more than (i) __________ and prefer to read news that is consistent with their beliefs, then (ii) __________ is not a journalistic flaw, but, rather, a cultivated feature. In a competitive news market, producers can use slant to differentiate their products and stave off price competition.

Blank 2

One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derision; appositeness; willingness to flatter

答案：C, F　prevalent; widespread

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　failure; indifference to

Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) __________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) __________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　originality; avant-garde; abandon

答案：D, F　misrepresent; underestimate

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　diffident; assertiveness

#258 TC TRIPLE
Nineteenth-century England presents something of (i) __________, at least to those who believe that (ii) __________ best promotes business ethics. The defining characteristic of English company law at that time was its extreme degree of permissiveness: laissez-faire ruled in Parliament, caveat emptor in the courts, creating the ideal breeding ground for fraud and dishonesty. Yet these were the years when London became the financial center of the world and established its status as a (iii) __________ place to do business.Nineteenth-century England presents something of (i) __________, at least to those who believe that (ii) __________ best promotes business ethics. The defining characteristic of English company law at that time was its extreme degree of permissiveness: laissez-faire ruled in Parliament, caveat emptor in the courts, creating the ideal breeding ground for fraud and dishonesty. Yet these were the years when London became the financial center of the world and established its status as a (iii) __________ place to do business.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　an enigma; regulation; comparatively safe
fraud
fraud: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　impugn; fruitless; assured

Throughout much of the twentieth century, common scientific sense seemed to dictate that animals could not make a choice based on rational or aesthetic criteria. Such choices were (i) __________ the mental capacity of humans. Scientists who (ii) __________ this animal-human cognitive division were often accused of anthropomorphism.Throughout much of the twentieth century, common scientific sense seemed to dictate that animals could not make a choice based on rational or aesthetic criteria. Such choices were (i) __________ the mental capacity of humans. Scientists who (ii) __________ this animal-human cognitive division were often accused of anthropomorphism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　reserved for; transgressed
inconsistent

#262 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
tendentious
tendentious: standard unit headword

However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. When native species do decline after nonnative species are introduced, the reason may be a human-caused habitat alteration that affects native and nonnative species (iii) __________.When the introduction of a nonnative species into a habitat correlates with the decline of a species native to that habitat, observers may (i) __________ a causal relationship. Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：C, E, H　infer; unusual; differentially

However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. The fishing industry has (i) __________ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)__________; nevertheless, the country's vast size and the isolation of many of its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)__________. This human pressure has (iii)__________ the South China tiger.China's rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People's Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)__________; nevertheless, the country's vast size and the isolation of many of its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)__________. This human pressure has (iii)__________ the South China tiger.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　implemented; can grow unchecked; doomed
celebratecelebrated
celebrate: standard unit headword | celebrated: standard unit headword
#307 SE
In the northeastern United States, beaver populations had been critically reduced or even __________ in large areas at the end of nineteenth century; as a result, several states instituted prohibitions on beaver trapping.In the northeastern United States, beaver populations had been critically reduced or even __________ in large areas at the end of nineteenth century; as a result, several states instituted prohibitions on beaver trapping.
答案：B, C　extirpated; eliminated
underrate
underrate: standard unit headword
#308 TC DOUBLE
The fact that the novelist strongly preferred to live a modern urban life has been (i) __________ by the successful efforts of a small number of influential critics to (ii) __________ the idea that her life and work were dominated by pastoral idealism.The fact that the novelist strongly preferred to live a modern urban life has been (i) __________ by the successful efforts of a small number of influential critics to (ii) __________ the idea that her life and work were dominated by pastoral idealism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　occluded; establish

introduce: standard unit headword
#309 TC TRIPLE
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools--quantum mechanics and streams of individual photons--to shut out (i) __________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools--quantum mechanics and streams of individual photons--to shut out (i) __________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane

celebrate: standard unit headword | celebrated: standard unit headword
#310 SE
The cat known to researchers as M-120—beefy, audacious, and apparently smart enough to spot a free lunch—is perhaps the world's least __________ lynx: the scientists catch him several times a year.The cat known to researchers as M-120—beefy, audacious, and apparently smart enough to spot a free lunch—is perhaps the world's least __________ lynx: the scientists catch him several times a year.
答案：B, E　evasive; elusive
intrepid
intrepid: standard unit headword
#311 TC DOUBLE
In the mid-1990s, researchers were aware that individuals and subpopulations vary in their susceptibilities to environmental pollutants. However, environmental health scientists, hoping to identify general trends, tended to view these variations as (i) __________ and used methods, in both human and animal studies, to (ii) __________.In the mid-1990s, researchers were aware that individuals and subpopulations vary in their susceptibilities to environmental pollutants. However, environmental health scientists, hoping to identify general trends, tended to view these variations as (i) __________ and used methods, in both human and animal studies, to (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　distractions; minimize their effect
measure
measure: standard unit headword
#312 TC TRIPLE
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; apt; impugned
puzzling
puzzling: standard unit headword
#313 SE
People’s decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal consideration and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often __________: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.People’s decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal consideration and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often __________: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.
答案：B, F　flummoxed; confounded
inconsistent
inconsistent: standard unit headword
#314 SE
We do not always use words in accordance with their dictionary definitions, for meaning often fluctuates with context; that does not mean, however, that we are completely __________ in how we use language.We do not always use words in accordance with their dictionary definitions, for meaning often fluctuates with context; that does not mean, however, that we are completely __________ in how we use language.
答案：B, E　unconstrained; free

rote: standard unit headword

Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)__________. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains - even the air we breathe - seem the norm and therefore (iii)__________.Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)__________. Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)__________. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains - even the air we breathe - seem the norm and therefore (iii)__________.

Blank 2

答案：B, E, G　atypical; permanent; eternal

#319 TC TRIPLE
Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　utopia; prompting; the ideal location for one to better oneself

答案：C, E　exhaustive; comprehensive

#321 TC TRIPLE
It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
formality
formality: standard unit headword
#322 SE
In establishing that the dust she had observed constitutes two percent of the mass in the quadrant, the astronomer showed that the dust's extreme visual prominence __________ its relatively minor contribution to the total mass of the region.In establishing that the dust she had observed constitutes two percent of the mass in the quadrant, the astronomer showed that the dust's extreme visual prominence __________ its relatively minor contribution to the total mass of the region.
答案：A, B　belies; masks

The major (ii)__________ urbanization in the United States, the railway fell victim to it. Once the overwhelming majority of nonelective journeys were either very long or very short, it became (iii)__________ for people to undertake them in planes or cars.By sucking people into towns and draining the countryside of communities and workers, the passenger train (i)__________ its reason for being: moving people between country districts and urban centers. The major (ii)__________ urbanization in the United States, the railway fell victim to it. Once the overwhelming majority of nonelective journeys were either very long or very short, it became (iii)__________ for people to undertake them in planes or cars.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　destroyed; facilitator of; sensible

答案：B, F　shortcoming; fault
