#325 TC TRIPLE
For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　accessible; proliferation of detail; oversimplifying

exposition: standard unit headword
#326 SE
Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two share a family history.Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two share a family history.
答案：D, E　preclude; rule out

Their goal, after all, is to (i) __________ the workings of that which we find comic, not to (ii) __________ laughter, and understanding why jokes are funny is not in itself (iii) __________.

答案：B, E, G　analyze; occasion; amusing

Belief in the harmful effects of the drug has all the hallmarks of a viewpoint that is not (i) __________ even though the notion that the drug has negative consequences has been (ii) __________ multiple epidemiological studies over the past decade. Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.Belief in the harmful effects of the drug has all the hallmarks of a viewpoint that is not (i) __________ even though the notion that the drug has negative consequences has been (ii) __________ multiple epidemiological studies over the past decade. Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amenable to refutation; upended by; persisted

cite: standard unit headword

Very few companies take the trouble to discover where the wood in their products originates, consumers do not demand this information, and consequently __________ regarding illicit timber has become the norm.Very few companies take the trouble to discover where the wood in their products originates, consumers do not demand this information, and consequently __________ regarding illicit timber has become the norm.
答案：B, F　indifference; apathy

A certain (i) __________ governs the way most of us think about routines. A routine is sometimes conceived as constraining--a self-made prison of inflexible habits that prevent people from changing their lives. But routine is also seen as (ii) __________: activities that become routine (iii) __________, allowing us to expend energy on other things.A certain (i) __________ governs the way most of us think about routines. A routine is sometimes conceived as constraining--a self-made prison of inflexible habits that prevent people from changing their lives. But routine is also seen as (ii) __________: activities that become routine (iii) __________, allowing us to expend energy on other things.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　polarity; liberating; demand less attention

Among the rising generation of concert pianists, a (i)__________ approach to performance is sadly (ii)__________. Too often, young pianists have acquired such an approach from their teachers, the very people who should be pointing out that the flailing arm, the contorted brow, the bulging eye, and the balletic upper torso are (iii)__________ work in a business best devoted to doing the most with the least.Among the rising generation of concert pianists, a (i)__________ approach to performance is sadly (ii)__________. Too often, young pianists have acquired such an approach from their teachers, the very people who should be pointing out that the flailing arm, the contorted brow, the bulging eye, and the balletic upper torso are (iii)__________ work in a business best devoted to doing the most with the least.

Blank 3

Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) __________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) __________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) __________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading

Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey's book is representative of a heightened sense of (i) __________. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　candor; antidote to; banal notion

Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism

overrate: standard unit headword

Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.
答案：C, F　prudent; circumspect

The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.Computers triumphed at chess not by (i) __________ human thought, but by playing like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　aping; instructive; escaped

Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.
答案：B, F　challenged; flouted

#349 TC TRIPLE
Until recently, historians of science treated fundamental research as (i) __________ pure science--that is, both were taken to refer to scientific investigations (ii) __________ technological and/or commercial objectives. Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.Until recently, historians of science treated fundamental research as (i) __________ pure science--that is, both were taken to refer to scientific investigations (ii) __________ technological and/or commercial objectives. Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　synonymous with; free from; appropriated by
objectionable
objectionable: standard unit headword
#350 SE
The Czech surrealist artist Toyen remains something of __________ in the art world despite the publication of an important monograph on her work in conjunction with a major retrospective exhibition.The Czech surrealist artist Toyen remains something of __________ in the art world despite the publication of an important monograph on her work in conjunction with a major retrospective exhibition.
答案：A, E　an enigma; a mystery
celebrity
celebrity: standard unit headword
#351 TC TRIPLE
Science is inherently (i) __________. Whether (ii) __________ a long-standing idea or showing disdain for received political wisdom, the scientific ethic, which demands that the scientist follow the evidence wherever it leads, is a threat to (iii) __________ of all kinds.Science is inherently (i) __________. Whether (ii) __________ a long-standing idea or showing disdain for received political wisdom, the scientific ethic, which demands that the scientist follow the evidence wherever it leads, is a threat to (iii) __________ of all kinds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　subversive; overturning; authorities
substantiate
substantiate: standard unit headword
#352 TC TRIPLE
Many writers are (i) __________ the term "unauthorized biography" because it sounds so (ii) __________. Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.Many writers are (i) __________ the term "unauthorized biography" because it sounds so (ii) __________. Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　uncomfortable with; nefarious; an invasion of
platitudinous
platitudinous: standard unit headword
#353 SE
Using elaborate costumes and lighting, dancer Loie Fuller transformed herself multiple times during a performance, thereby emphasizing the __________ of character.Using elaborate costumes and lighting, dancer Loie Fuller transformed herself multiple times during a performance, thereby emphasizing the __________ of character.
答案：A, F　fluidity; mutability
universality
universality: standard unit headword
#354 TC TRIPLE
Every few years, as a reviewer, one encounters a novel whose (i) __________ are so many in number, and so (ii) __________, that to correct them fully would produce a text that exceeded the novel itself in length. Faced with such a book, one wishes only to let it slip quietly to the seabed of culture, there to join thousands of other (iii) __________ books in their slow disintegration.Every few years, as a reviewer, one encounters a novel whose (i) __________ are so many in number, and so (ii) __________, that to correct them fully would produce a text that exceeded the novel itself in length. Faced with such a book, one wishes only to let it slip quietly to the seabed of culture, there to join thousands of other (iii) __________ books in their slow disintegration.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　ineptitudes; thoroughgoing; incompetent
delightful
delightful: standard unit headword
#355 SE
One of the reporter's central themes is that health care concerns were a secondary consideration to the reformers in their drive to limit if not completely __________ the consumption of cigarettes.One of the reporter's central themes is that health care concerns were a secondary consideration to the reformers in their drive to limit if not completely __________ the consumption of cigarettes.
答案：B, E　proscribe; disallow
impair
impair: standard unit headword
#356 SE
In contrast to the novel's scenic realism and precise characterization is its persistent philosophical __________.In contrast to the novel's scenic realism and precise characterization is its persistent philosophical __________.
答案：B, C　abstraction; generality
naturalism
naturalism: standard unit headword
#357 TC TRIPLE
During the past decade, there has been a quiet (i) __________ research into symbioses involving microorganisms: purely descriptive and speculative work has given way to investigations generating data that allow for (ii) __________ interpretation and thus (iii) __________ unfounded speculation.During the past decade, there has been a quiet (i) __________ research into symbioses involving microorganisms: purely descriptive and speculative work has given way to investigations generating data that allow for (ii) __________ interpretation and thus (iii) __________ unfounded speculation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　change in; defensible; reduce the tendency toward
validate
validate: standard unit headword
#358 SE
A successful deception must fulfill some need or desire in its audience if it is to foster credulity even in the face of its own __________ features.A successful deception must fulfill some need or desire in its audience if it is to foster credulity even in the face of its own __________ features.
答案：A, E　implausible; unlikely
fruitless
fruitless: standard unit headword
#359 TC TRIPLE
Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　churlish; approbation; consistently perspicacious
tendentious
tendentious: standard unit headword
#360 TC TRIPLE
It is an accepted assumption of the theater that every playwright has an unproduced play stuck away in a drawer somewhere. The same assumption holds that such plays are usually youthful efforts and that all urges to take them out of the drawer should be (i) __________, lest they bring (ii) __________ to the playwright and (iii) __________ to admirers.It is an accepted assumption of the theater that every playwright has an unproduced play stuck away in a drawer somewhere. The same assumption holds that such plays are usually youthful efforts and that all urges to take them out of the drawer should be (i) __________, lest they bring (ii) __________ to the playwright and (iii) __________ to admirers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　repressed; embarrassment; distress
solace
solace: standard unit headword
#361 SE
The senator complained that the president's argument against the provision was __________, gaining its apparent plausibility from a distorted presentation of the relevant data.The senator complained that the president's argument against the provision was __________, gaining its apparent plausibility from a distorted presentation of the relevant data.
答案：B, C　specious; deceptive
platitudinous
platitudinous: standard unit headword
#362 SE
Suburbanization arguably ushered in numerous changes in the way many people in the United States lived: the suburbs __________ a society based on unprecedented consumption and intense privacy.Suburbanization arguably ushered in numerous changes in the way many people in the United States lived: the suburbs __________ a society based on unprecedented consumption and intense privacy.
答案：C, D　inaugurated; engendered
validate
validate: standard unit headword
#363 SE
Noguchi had an unshakable belief in the __________ of art: he thought that artists should inject their knowledge of form into the everyday, practical designs of industry and commerce.Noguchi had an unshakable belief in the __________ of art: he thought that artists should inject their knowledge of form into the everyday, practical designs of industry and commerce.
答案：B, D　functionality; utility
universality
universality: standard unit headword
#364 SE
Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.
答案：B, C　fractious; restive
tractablecommitment
tractable: standard unit headword | commitment: standard unit headword
#365 SE
Although Wynne claims to recognize that __________ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.Although Wynne claims to recognize that __________ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.
答案：C, F　insufficient; meager
conclusivedefinitive
conclusive: standard unit headword | definitive: standard unit headword
#366 SE
Although Wayne claims to recognize that __________ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.Although Wayne claims to recognize that __________ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.
答案：C, F　insufficient; meager
conclusivedefinitive
conclusive: standard unit headword | definitive: standard unit headword
